primary_complaint: Worsening left hip pain, evaluate for surgical intervention
functional_limitations: Difficulty ambulating distances greater than 200 feet

denial_risk_flags.label: X-Ray Results Pending at Submission
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The x-ray ordered at today's visit (01/30/2025) has results pending, and no completed imaging is available to confirm the severity of M16.12. United Healthcare requires completed radiographic evidence of osteoarthritis before authorizing CPT 27130.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Do not submit the prior authorization until the left hip x-ray results are finalized and documented in the chart. Include the radiology report with the PA submission.

denial_risk_flags.label: Imaging Not Yet Complete
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Payers require completed imaging results before authorizing surgical procedures. Both x-ray and MRI are either pending or not yet ordered as of 01/30/2025.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Do not submit until imaging results are available and documented.

denial_risk_flags.label: No Physical Therapy Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: No formal physical therapy is documented in the referral. United Healthcare typically requires documented failure of supervised physical therapy (minimum 6 weeks) prior to approving CPT 27130 total hip arthroplasty.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Initiate and document a formal physical therapy program of at least 6 weeks duration, or obtain records from prior PT if completed elsewhere, before submitting the PA.

denial_risk_flags.label: Insufficient Conservative Care
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Only 1 treatment documented (OTC pain medication with no duration or outcome). Payers for CPT 27130 typically require documented failure of physical therapy (minimum 6 weeks), NSAIDs, and at least one injection before approving surgical intervention.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Obtain records documenting additional conservative treatments or initiate and document further conservative care before submission.

denial_risk_flags.label: OTC Medication — No Duration or Outcome
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The only documented conservative treatment is OTC pain medication with no duration, specific medication name, dosage, or outcome recorded. Payers require specific treatment details to evaluate adequacy of conservative care.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Document the specific OTC medication used (e.g., ibuprofen, naproxen), the dosage, duration of use, and the patient's response or reason for failure.

denial_risk_flags.label: No Corticosteroid or Injection Therapy Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: United Healthcare typically requires at least one intra-articular injection (corticosteroid or hyaluronic acid) as part of conservative care failure documentation before approving total hip arthroplasty (CPT 27130).
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Document whether the patient has received or was offered intra-articular hip injections, and include outcome and dates if performed.

denial_risk_flags.label: Symptom Duration Vague — No Specific Timeframe
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Symptom duration is documented only as 'over the past several months,' which is imprecise. Payers may require a specific duration to assess chronicity and medical necessity for surgical intervention.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Clarify and document the exact onset date or specific duration of left hip pain (e.g., '8 months,' 'since [DATE]') in the chart addendum.

denial_risk_flags.label: Preliminary Chart — Pre-Op Workup Incomplete
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The chart explicitly states this is preliminary referral documentation with a full pre-op workup pending. Submitting a PA on incomplete documentation increases the risk of denial due to insufficient medical necessity evidence.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Complete the pre-operative workup including imaging, ASA classification, and full clinical assessment before submitting the prior authorization to United Healthcare.

denial_risk_flags.label: No ASA Classification Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: ASA physical status classification is absent from the chart. While not always required for PA approval, United Healthcare may request it as part of surgical candidacy documentation for CPT 27130.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Document the patient's ASA classification in the pre-operative assessment prior to PA submission.

denial_risk_flags.label: No Surgical Approach Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: No surgical approach (e.g., anterior, posterior, lateral) is specified for the planned total hip arthroplasty. Some payers request this detail as part of the procedure authorization.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Document the planned surgical approach (e.g., direct anterior approach) in the operative plan section of the PA submission.

diagnosis_codes.note: ICD-10 code M16.12 (primary osteoarthritis, left hip) is explicitly documented and appropriate for CPT 27130.

conservative_treatments_named.note: Only one vague conservative treatment (OTC pain medication) is named; no PT, injections, or NSAIDs by name are documented.

conservative_treatment_duration.note: No duration is documented for any conservative treatment attempted.

imaging_findings.note: No completed imaging results are available; x-ray is pending and MRI has not been ordered.

functional_limitations.note: Two specific functional limitations are documented: ambulation limited to 200 feet and inability to perform prolonged standing.

surgical_approach.note: No surgical approach is documented for the planned total hip arthroplasty.

cpt_code_valid.note: CPT 27130 is explicitly stated and is the correct code for total hip arthroplasty.

symptom_duration.note: Symptom duration is documented only as 'over the past several months,' which is too vague to meet payer specificity requirements.

hard_blocks.label: Imaging Findings
hard_blocks.message: No imaging results are documented. Payers require objective imaging evidence to authorize surgical intervention.

soft_warnings.label: Surgical Approach
soft_warnings.message: Anatomical approach details strengthen the surgical indication but can be inferred.
